chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Blood Money for Fingers, Teeth, and Bones

text: 5302 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى اَلْخَزَّازُ عَنْ غِيَاثِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "فِي اَلْإِصْبَعِ اَلزَّائِدَةِ إِذَا قُطِعَتْ ثُلُثُ دِيَةِ اَلصَّحِيحَةِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.5302 - Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said that if an extra (supernumerary) finger is cut off, then one-third of the blood money (diyyah) of a normal finger is applicable.
